office: House Committee on the Judiciary (Democratic Staff)
description:
The Democratic staff of the House Committee on the Judiciary is seeking a digital intern for a spring position. This digital internship program includes the production of high-quality graphics, videos, and demonstratives, as well as rapid response work, post production editing, and livestreaming hybrid/virtual hearings and events.

Candidates must possess STRONG technical skills, advanced creativity, and a high level of attention to detail. Experience in Webex, OBS, Adobe Photoshop, Illustrator, Premiere Pro, After Effects, Lightroom, InDesign, and Audition a plus. Experience in HTML, CSS, hard-skills technology and big-data analytics a serious plus. Must be a team player and have a strong work ethic. Willingness to take initiative, generate creative ideas, and the ability to work under deadline pressure a must.

The House Committee on the Judiciary's Democratic staff internship program will be providing stipends to spring interns, with additional funds provided to interns with additional demonstrated financial need. The Committee strives to ensure that our internship class reflects the diversity of the country's population; candidates from minority backgrounds are strongly encouraged to apply.

COVID-19 update: Currently, some of our spring internship program will be conducted remotely. However, we ask that all applicants be available to intern in Washington, D.C., as some tasks will be done on site.
responsibilities: Produce high-quality graphics, videos, and demonstratives
qualifications: Possess strong technical skills
Demonstrate advanced creativity
Maintain a high level of attention to detail
Experience in Webex, OBS, Adobe Photoshop, Illustrator, Premiere Pro, After Effects, Lightroom, InDesign, and Audition preferred
Experience in HTML, CSS, hard-skills technology, and big-data analytics highly preferred
Be a team player with a strong work ethic
Willingness to take initiative and generate creative ideas
Ability to work under deadline pressure
how_to_apply: Email portfolio and resume to JudPress.Intern@mail.house.gov with DIGITAL INTERN in the subject line. Digital portfolio, including design and technical work, must be included to be considered for the position.
salary_info: Stipends provided to spring interns, with additional funds provided to interns with additional demonstrated financial need
equal_opportunity: The Committee does not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sex (including marital or parental status, sexual orientation, and gender identity), disability, age, or national origin.

office: House Committee on the Judiciary (Democratic Staff)
description:
The House Judiciary Committee Democratic staff are looking for interns for the summer semester. The House Committee on the Judiciary's staff internship program offers undergraduate students, law students, graduate students, and recent graduates an opportunity to experience the operation of a congressional committee firsthand and learn about the important role that our Committee plays in the legislative process.

The Judiciary Committee has jurisdiction over a wide range of legislative and oversight issues, including oversight of the U.S. Department of Justice, constitutional law, federal criminal law, voting rights, immigration and naturalization, intellectual property law, and antitrust law.

Intern assignments will vary depending on the work of the Judiciary Committee each semester.

COVID-19 update: Due to the COVID-19 pandemic the Committee is currently planning on having a remote internship for the summer of 2021. However, public health developments could necessitate the return of an in-person internship in Washington, D.C. Therefore, we ask that all applicants be available to intern in Washington, D.C. if it is safe to do so.
responsibilities: Researching policy issues under the committee's jurisdiction
Attending briefings and hearings on the Hill
Supporting committee clerks and professional staff members
Providing administrative support, including answering calls, delivering correspondence, and setting up the hearing room
qualifications: Undergraduate, law, or graduate students
Recent graduates
Availability to intern in Washington, D.C. if safe to do so
how_to_apply: To learn more, please apply here: https://judiciary.house.gov/about/internship-opportunities.htm. The deadline to apply for the summer semester is 2021-02-15.
equal_opportunity: The Committee does not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sex (including marital or parental status, sexual orientation, and gender identity), disability, age, or national origin.

description:
The House Judiciary Committee Democratic staff are looking for an intern to work with our Crime, Terrorism, and Homeland Security subcommittee this spring, with an ideal start date of early February.

The House Committee on the Judiciary's staff internship program offers undergraduate students, law students, graduate students, and recent graduates an opportunity to experience the operation of a congressional committee firsthand and learn about the important role that our Committee plays in the legislative process.

The Subcommittee on Crime, Terrorism, and Homeland Security has jurisdiction over the Federal Criminal Code, drug enforcement, sentencing, parole and pardons, internal and homeland security, the Federal Rules of Criminal Procedure, prisons, and criminal law enforcement.

Intern assignments will vary depending on the work of the Judiciary Committee each semester. Examples of potential responsibilities include:

- Researching policy issues under the committee's jurisdiction
- Conducting legal research
- Attending briefings and hearings on the Hill
- Drafting memos
- Supporting committee counsels
- Supporting committee clerks and professional staff members
- Providing administrative support, including answering calls, delivering correspondence, and setting up the hearing room.

The Committee strives to ensure that our internship class reflects the diversity of the country's population; candidates from minority backgrounds are strongly encouraged to apply. The Committee does not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sex (including marital or parental status, sexual orientation, and gender identity), disability, age, or national origin. The committee is providing stipends to spring interns.

Applications will be accepted on a rolling basis. To apply, please submit a one-page resume, one-page cover letter (including your pronouns, ideal start and end dates, and availability to work each weekday), writing sample, and a short statement in response to the prompt below to HJC.Internship@mail.house.gov with the subject line "Crime Subcommittee Internship"

Prompt: Please attach a short statement (no more than 500 words) detailing how your lived experience will enhance your ability to contribute to this committee. This prompt is open-ended and you may respond by writing about whatever is most meaningful to you. Examples of topics this statement could cover include your upbringing, a significant challenge you have overcome, or any topic that you believe is relevant.

COVID-19 update: Due to the COVID-19 pandemic the Committee is currently working virtually. However, public health developments could necessitate the return of an in-person internship in Washington, D.C. Therefore, we ask that all applicants be available to intern in Washington, D.C. if it is safe to do so.
responsibilities: Researching policy issues under the committee's jurisdiction
Attending briefings and hearings on the Hill
Supporting committee clerks and professional staff members
Providing administrative support, including answering calls, delivering correspondence, and setting up the hearing room
qualifications: Undergraduate students, law students, graduate students, or recent graduates
Availability to work each weekday
Ability to intern in Washington, D.C. if required due to public health developments
how_to_apply: Submit a one-page resume, one-page cover letter (including your pronouns, ideal start and end dates, and availability to work each weekday), writing sample, and a short statement in response to the prompt to HJC.Internship@mail.house.gov with the subject line "Crime Subcommittee Internship". The statement should be no more than 500 words and detail how your lived experience will enhance your ability to contribute to this committee.
equal_opportunity: The Committee does not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sex (including marital or parental status, sexual orientation, and gender identity), disability, age, or national origin.

description:
Congressman Alcee L. Hastings (FL-20) is seeking highly motivated, detail-oriented, and energetic applicants for a full or part-time unpaid internship in Washington, D.C. to begin immediately.

Qualified applicants have strong writing and communication skills, the ability to manage multiple tasks, and the willingness to learn. Primary responsibilities include answering phones, conducting legislative research and drafting memos, and assisting in managing the Congressman's official correspondence.

Congressman Hastings supports students requesting credit for their internship through their school or university. To apply please visit https://alceehastings.house.gov/forms/internships/. NO phone calls or drop-ins please.

This is an in-person internship. Applicants must be willing to wear a mask and adhere to strict social distancing and sanitation guidelines. Women and minorities are strongly encouraged to apply.
responsibilities: Conducting legislative research and drafting memos
Assisting in managing the Congressman's official correspondence
qualifications: Strong writing and communication skills
Ability to manage multiple tasks
Willingness to learn
Must be willing to wear a mask and adhere to strict social distancing and sanitation guidelines
how_to_apply: To apply please visit https://alceehastings.house.gov/forms/internships/. NO phone calls or drop-ins please.
equal_opportunity: Women and minorities are strongly encouraged to apply.

description:
The Office of Congressman Kweisi Mfume (MD-07) is seeking a motivated, detail-oriented intern to work remotely in a fast-paced Congressional office. Intern responsibilities include answering phones, researching legislation, attending virtual hearings and briefings, and answering constituent letters on various issues before the House. Interns will also assist in various administrative tasks as needed. As a result, the intern will learn about the legislative process and the many other functions of a congressional office.

The term of this paid internship will be from January to April 2021. Due to the COVID-19 pandemic, interns will solely work remotely for the duration of the internship and be provided the appropriate work equipment. The position is part-time (~20 hours a week).
responsibilities: Answering constituent letters on various issues before the House
Assisting in various administrative tasks as needed
qualifications: Motivated and detail-oriented
District and Maryland ties encouraged but not required
how_to_apply: Interested applicants should email a cover letter, resume, short writing sample, and dates of availability to Md07employment@mail.house.gov with "Spring 2021 Congressional Internship" in the subject line. The deadline to submit applications is January 20th at 5:00 p.m. ET. No phone calls, follow-ups or drop-ins, please.
equal_opportunity: All applicants will be considered without regard to race, color, national origin, religion, sex, gender identity, disability, or age. Women, LGBTQ+, and minority candidates are encouraged to apply.

description:
The Office of Congressman Kweisi Mfume (MD-07) is seeking a Digital Press Intern to assist with a fast-paced communications operation for Spring 2021. This is a paid, part-time position (~20 hours a week). Qualified candidates will have strong writing, video editing, social media, graphic design, and photography skills, and be able to work on tight deadlines.

Due to the COVID-19 pandemic, interns will solely work remotely for the duration of the internship and be provided the appropriate work equipment. The selected candidate will report to the Communications Director.

District and Maryland ties are encouraged but not required. No phone calls, follow-ups or drop-ins, please.
responsibilities: Performing other press and administrative tasks as necessary
qualifications: Strong writing skills
Video editing skills
Social media skills
Graphic design skills
Photography skills
Ability to work on tight deadlines
Familiarity with programs such as Adobe Premiere Pro, Adobe Photoshop, Adobe InDesign, and Canva
Ability to compile and interpret social media analytics
District and Maryland ties encouraged but not required
how_to_apply: Interested applicants should email a cover letter, resume, portfolio of created content, and dates of availability to Md07employment@mail.house.gov with "Spring 2021 Digital Press Internship" in the subject line. The deadline to submit applications is January 20th at 5:00 p.m. ET.
salary_info: Paid, part-time position (~20 hours a week)
equal_opportunity: Women, LGBTQ+, and minority candidates are encouraged to apply. All applicants will be considered without regard to race, color, national origin, religion, sex, gender identity, disability, or age.

office: House Foreign Affairs Committee Majority Staff
description: The House Foreign Affairs Committee Majority Staff is currently looking for interns for the Spring 2021 semester. Interns will be responsible for providing staff members with policy and administrative support. Duties may include answering phones, compiling press clips, attending staff briefings, performing legislative and policy research, drafting memoranda and other written materials, and a variety of other tasks. Candidates should be team-oriented, resourceful, upbeat people who have skills beyond research and require little supervision. This internship is open to undergraduates (juniors and seniors), recent graduates, and Master's and law students. Minorities, women, and LGBTQ persons are strongly encouraged to apply.
responsibilities: Provide staff members with policy and administrative support
Draft memoranda and other written materials
Perform a variety of other tasks
qualifications: Team-oriented, resourceful, and upbeat
Skills beyond research
Ability to work with little supervision
Undergraduates (juniors and seniors), recent graduates, or Master's and law students
how_to_apply: Submit a one-page resume and cover letter in PDF format to hcfa.democrat@mail.house.gov and include 'HFAC Internship Application - Spring 2021' in the subject line of the email.
equal_opportunity: Minorities, women, and LGBTQ persons are strongly encouraged to apply.
